---
title: "Small Butterwort (Pinguicula pumila)"
slug: "pinguicula-pumila"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621857708/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Small Butterwort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Lamiales"
tax_family: "Lentibulariaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Lamiales", "Lentibulariaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621857708/medium.jpg" alt="Small Butterwort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jimw3">
jimw3</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341579214">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Lamiales
-   **Family:** Lentibulariaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=166889)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common/Uncommon species in Florida found in moist, nutrient-poor habitats.

## Natural history notes

A tiny carnivorous plant characterized by its small basal rosette of sticky leaves and delicate yellow to pale purple flowers borne on slender stalks. In Florida, it is found in moist, nutrient-poor habitats such as bogs, wet flatwoods, and seepage areas. It typically blooms during the cooler to warmer months and captures small insects on its sticky leaves to supplement nutrients.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/166889-Pinguicula-pumila)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-pinguicula-pumila/)
